mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no predictive relationship between rate changes and equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, invalidating the negative predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of autos and light trucks and industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply expansion, which compresses valuation multiples and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation readings force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which triggers immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in tighter financial conditions over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during high-inflation periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the negative relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, indicating no predictive power for equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls signal abrupt labor market contraction and reduced household income, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in over 60% of months following a payroll drop exceeding 50,000.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion and durable consumer demand, which supports equity risk premiums and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and GDPC1 signal shows a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Prolonged federal funds rate stability combined with elevated unemployment creates policy uncertainty and stagflation fears, triggering short-term equity liquidation and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains unchanged for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during prolonged rate stability periods show no significant negative drift with a p-value greater than 0.10.
